news.headline: Skyharbour's partner North Shore Uranium signs exploration agreement with English Rivers First Nation for Falcon property. North Shore can acquire 80% by issuing shares worth CAD $1.23M, cash payments of $525k to Skyharbour, and $3.55M in exploration costs.
news.summary: Vancouver, BC, March 20, 2025 - Skyharbour Resources Ltd. announced that its partner, North Shore Uranium, has signed an exploration agreement with the English River First Nation in Saskatchewan. The agreement focuses on collaboration at the Falcon property in the Athabasca Basin. North Shore has the opportunity to acquire an initial 80% interest in Falcon by issuing common shares worth CAD $1,225,000, making cash payments of $525,000 to Skyharbour, and spending $3,550,000 on exploration on the property during the earn-in period.

S. Korean PPI YoY Actual 1.5% (Forecast -, Previous 1.7%)
DTCC supports Ethereum's ERC3643 token standard for permissioned securities, joins ERC3643 Association. DTCC settles $3 quadrillion transactions in 2023.
news.summary: The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC) has committed to promoting Ethereum’s ERC-3643 standard for permissioned securities tokens and has joined the ERC3643 Association. This move highlights US regulators' acceptance of tokenization after President Donald Trump's pledge to make America the "world’s crypto capital." The DTCC settled most US securities transactions in 2023, totaling $3 quadrillion. ERC-3643, also known as the T-REX Protocol, is an open-source suite enabling the issuance, management, and transfer of permissioned tokens even on permissionless blockchains. The DTCC has been an early mover in adopting blockchain technology, testing settling tokenized US Treasury Bills and piloting private asset tokenization. In February, they launched ComposerX to streamline token creation and settlement for regulated US financial institutions, in line with the CFTC’s plans for onchain settlement in derivatives markets.

news.headline: White House orders closure of Education Dept. to start shutting down – student loan debts not canceled.
news.summary: An executive order from the White House has directed the secretary of education to commence the process of closing down the department. However, it does not include provisions for cancelling any outstanding debt.

news.headline: Quantum Computing Inc (NASDAQ:QUBT) Q4 revenue at $62,000, EPS loss at 47 cents. Shares fall in after-hours trading.
news.summary: Quantum Computing Inc (NASDAQ:QUBT) saw a decline in shares during after-hours trading following the release of its fourth-quarter results. In Q4, the company reported revenue of $62,000, falling short of estimates of $200,000. The EPS for the quarter was a loss of 47 cents, significantly higher than the expected loss of five cents. Operating expenses for the fourth quarter stood at $8.9 million, showing an increase from $6.6 million from the previous year. Quantum Computing ended the quarter with $78.9 million in cash and cash equivalents. For more details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: SoftBank acquires Ampere, signaling bright future for Ed-Tech sector.
news.summary: In the latest updates, it has been reported that there has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the city. The total number of confirmed cases has now reached 10,000, with an additional 500 new cases reported in the last 24 hours alone. This surge in infections has put a strain on the healthcare system, with hospitals nearing full capacity. Health officials are urging the public to follow safety guidelines and get vaccinated to help curb the spread of the virus. In response to the rising cases, authorities have implemented stricter lockdown measures to contain the outbreak. Residents are advised to stay at home as much as possible and avoid large gatherings. The situation is being closely monitored, and further updates will be provided as the situation develops.

news.headline: AstroNova (Nasdaq: ALOT) restructures to save $3 million annually with amended credit facility and preliminary fiscal Q4 results.
news.summary: In West Warwick, Rhode Island, AstroNova, Inc. (Nasdaq: ALOT), a prominent player in data visualization technologies, has achieved a waiver and amendment of its revolving credit facility and term loans with Bank of America. Alongside this, the Company disclosed early unaudited financial figures for its fiscal 2025 fourth quarter, which concluded on January 31, 2025. Notably, they introduced a restructuring initiative set to deliver an anticipated yearly cost reduction of $3 million.

news.headline: Equity Residential (NYSE: EQR) declares $0.6925 dividend per share for Q1, a 2.6% increase from 2024, payable on April 17, 2025.
news.summary: Equity Residential (NYSE: EQR) declared quarterly dividends for its common and preferred shares. The first quarter will see a $0.6925 per share dividend paid on April 17, 2025, to shareholders of record by March 31, 2025. This marks a 2.6% increase from the 2024 dividend, amounting to an annualized $2.77 per share. The company aims to provide long-term value to its shareholders.

news.headline: Northview Residential REIT declares March distribution & tax details for 2024 distributions; important numbers disclosed.
news.summary: The recent data shows a concerning increase in COVID-19 cases in the country, with over 50,000 new infections reported in the past week. Hospitalizations have also risen significantly, with approximately 4,000 people currently being treated for the virus. The number of deaths related to COVID-19 has also spiked, reaching a total of 700 in the last seven days. Authorities have attributed this surge to the highly transmissible Delta variant and have urged the public to adhere to safety measures such as mask-wearing and social distancing to curb the spread of the virus. Vaccination efforts are being ramped up in response to the escalating situation, with over 1 million vaccine doses administered in the past week.

news.headline: Dow Jones gains, Nvidia rises, Warren Buffett stock hits new high. China stocks fall, Cathie Wood picks beaten-down stocks.
news.summary: Today, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced an increase in stock prices, with Nvidia also seeing a rise in the market. In contrast, stocks in China saw a decline. Notably, prominent investor Cathie Wood opted to invest in more undervalued stocks. Additionally, Warren Buffett’s stock reached a new high. The details of these market movements were covered in a live broadcast by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Stock market hesitates, awaits bullish signal. Micron leads earnings movers. Dow Jones Futures in focus.
news.summary: On Thursday, there was a slight decrease in the stock market rally as investors are still waiting for a bullish signal. Micron emerged as the top performer among earnings movers in the late trading session. The Dow Jones Futures are at a crucial point, with the market on the verge of showing a bullish signal. This update comes amidst an eventful trading day, where important numbers fluctuated as investors closely monitored the market movements.

news.headline: Recent incidents of travelers denied entry to US are heightening concerns about crossing the border as an international visitor.
news.summary: In the past few weeks, there has been a notable increase in the number of cases where travelers have faced refusal to enter the United States, raising apprehension regarding the entry process at airports and other border points.

news.headline: Patriot Rail Company in Jacksonville wins 2025 USA TODAY Top Workplaces award for being a premier U.S. rail operator.
news.summary: In Jacksonville, Florida, on March 20, 2025, Patriot Rail Company, a leading U.S. provider of short line and regional freight railroads, transloading, and related rail services, was granted the esteemed 2025 USA TODAY Top Workplaces recognition. news.headline: Cryptocurrencies dip after FOMC impact: BTC at $84,401 (-1.4%), ETH at $1,975.75 (-2.6%), XRP at $2.43 (-1.9%) with trading updates.:initComponents
news.summary: Cryptocurrencies experienced a slight retracement in their gains following President Trump's address to cryptocurrency investors via a pre-recorded statement. Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) was priced at $84,401, representing a decrease of 1.4%. Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) saw a drop of 2.6% to $1,975.75. Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) decreased by 3.6% to $127.88, XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) fell by 1.9% to $2.43, Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) dropped by 2.7% to $0.1692, and Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) experienced a slight decline of 0.6% to $0.00001286.According to IntoTheBlock data, the large transaction volume remained stable at $45.9 billion, with a 1.8% increase in daily active addresses. Transactions exceeding $100,000 rose from 9,552 to 9,995 in a single day, while exchanges netflows decreased by 212.4%. Coinglass data revealed that 92,154 traders were liquidated in the past 24 hours, resulting in losses totaling $273.77 million.In other news, a Coinbase analyst expressed being 'cautiously optimistic' on the stock with a balanced risk/reward, with further details available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Nike Q3 earnings beat expectations with $11.27B revenue, 54 cents EPS. Revenue down 9% YoY, inventories down 2%. Regional sales decline noted.
news.summary: Nike Inc disclosed their financial performance for the third quarter of fiscal 2025 following the market closure on Thursday. The company reported total revenue for Q3 at $11.27 billion, surpassing the anticipated $11.01 billion as per analyst projections. Earnings for the quarter stood at 54 cents per share, exceeding the expected 28 cents per share. Despite the positive earnings report, there was a 9% decrease in total revenue compared to the previous year. Sales in different regions also saw declines:- North America experienced a 4% decrease- Greater China faced a 17% decline- Europe, Middle East & Africa saw a 10% dropThe Asia Pacific and Latin America regions were not detailed in the available excerpt.

news.headline: Members discussed efforts to enhance Treasury market resilience - US Treasury Statement.
Bitcoin's 3.6% volatility on Mar 19 reflects market uncertainty. Potential $115B savings via Gov Efficiency Dept. BTC at $84K.
news.summary: Bitcoin's volatility climbed to 3.6% on March 19, the highest since August 2024, as per data from CoinGlass, reflecting market uncertainty due to unknowns in the US economy. The Department of Government Efficiency, led by Elon Musk, claims to have saved $115 billion mainly through cost-cutting measures. Federal Open Market Committee announced leaving interest rates unchanged, possibly cutting twice in 2025. Since Trump's inauguration, Bitcoin's price fell 30% from $109,590 to $77,041. Recent price at $84,000 shows demand returning. Traders are adjusting to the possibility of fiscal tightening and stable or declining interest rates, affecting Bitcoin's sensitivity to economic cycles and liquidity shifts. President Trump's comments on Bitcoin and geopolitical tensions drive market reactions, including crypto, showing a complex interplay between policy and market dynamics.

news.headline: Trump plans to use wartime powers to increase critical mineral production, leading to a boost in national security.

Sunwing flight attendants protest Quebec City base closure by WestJet, urge retention. CUPE 8125 & ALPA demand action.
news.summary: Flight attendants at Sunwing, represented by CUPE 8125, are criticizing WestJet's move to shut down their Quebec City base following the acquisition and merger of operations with Sunwing in 2024. CUPE 8125 members, alongside Sunwing pilots from the Air Line Pilots Association, held a press conference on March 17 in Quebec City to urge WestJet to keep the base operational. Dominique Côté, an experienced Sunwing flight attendant with 18 years of service, emphasized the importance of retaining jobs in Quebec City.

Capstone Copper upsized Notes offering to $600M at 6.750% interest, originally planned for $500M.

Colgate-Palmolive raises dividend to $.52 per share, new rate $2.08 annually. Introduces $5 billion share repurchase program.
news.summary: In New York, the Board of Directors of Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) announced a raise in the quarterly common stock cash dividend from $.50 to $.52 per share. This increase will take effect in the second quarter of 2025. The Board stated that the dividend for the second quarter will be disbursed on May 15, 2025, to shareholders listed as of April 17, 2025. Annually, the new dividend rate amounts to $2.08, a rise from the previous $2.00 per share. Colgate-Palmolive Company has a history of consistent dividend payments.

Winger will succeed Natasha Cherednichenko, who exits the company after significant contributions since 2020. Winger aims to drive growth while maintaining financial discipline. news.headline: FHLB Dallas & First Federal Bank of Louisiana award $100K grant to Project Build a Future for heirship education.
news.summary: In Lake Charles, Louisiana, the Federal Home Loan Bank of Dallas (FHLB Dallas) and First Federal Bank of Louisiana are set to present a $100,000 grant to Project Build a Future (PBAF) during a check ceremony on March 27th. This grant, facilitated by FHLB Dallas member First Federal Bank of Louisiana, aims to address heirship issues in the community. The funds will support PBAF in collaborating with local experts to raise awareness and provide education on this critical topic.

news.headline: Rithm Property Trust sells 84,232 shares of Series C Preferred Stock, generating $2,105,800 in gross proceeds.
news.summary: Rithm Property Trust Inc. revealed the sale of 84,232 shares of its 9.875% Series C Fixed-to-Floating Rate Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock, under the NYSE ticker symbol RPT. This sale, part of a public offering, was made through the partial exercise of the over-allotment option to underwriters. The transaction generated $2,105,800 in gross proceeds, prior to deductions for underwriting discounts and other expenses.

news.headline: Curtiss-Wright awarded contracts to provide turret drive stabilization systems for Boxer Heavy Weapon Carriers and Lynx IFVs.
news.summary: Davidson, North Carolina - Curtiss-Wright has recently secured contracts to supply turret drive aiming and stabilization technology to Rheinmetall, intended for incorporation into ground vehicles in Germany and Hungary. The contracts signal a significant step forward for the company, as they look to bolster their presence in the defense industry. This development underscores the increasing demand for advanced defense systems, with the contracts highlighting a growing market for such technologies. The agreements are poised to enhance the capabilities of the German and Hungarian ground vehicles, with Curtiss-Wright at the helm of providing crucial technology to drive innovation in the sector.

news.headline: Luminar Technologies (NASDAQ:LAZR) reports Q4 losses of $1.26/share with revenue of $22.48M, beating estimates.
news.summary: Luminar Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: LAZR) announced its Q4 results after Thursday’s market close. The report revealed a loss of $1.26 per share, with quarterly revenue reaching $22.48 million. This revenue exceeded analyst expectations of $17.72 million and marked a slight increase from the $22.11 million revenue reported in the same quarter of the previous year. For more information, visit Benzinga.com for the full story on Darden Restaurants CEO's statements.

news.summary: On March 20, 2025, Sunoco LP (NYSE: SUN) revealed that a private offering of 6.250% senior notes due 2033 has been priced at 100%, with an aggregate principal amount of $1 billion. The offering involved the issuance of the notes totaling $1 billion.

news.headline: Market anticipates lower rates as Treasuries continue to rally after Fed news. #rates #bonds #economy
Former Newmont Executive David Copley to oversee the mining portfolio for US National Energy Dominance Council, according to two sources.

Bitcoin longs at Bitfinex reach 6-month high of 80,333 BTC ($6.92B). Leverage-driven price gains may not hold, despite profitable traders and low borrowing costs. OKX ratio favors longs while options market remains neutral, signaling no consensus for a BTC bull run amid US Fed concerns.
news.summary: Bullish Bitcoin positions using leverage on Bitfinex exchange have surged to their highest level in nearly six months, reaching 80,333 BTC on March 20, equivalent to $6.92 billion. The increase in Bitcoin margin longs since Feb. 20 has led to speculation about the sustainability of the current price gain. Despite the historical profitability of Bitcoin margin traders, an increase in leverage demand does not necessarily translate to upward pressure on Bitcoin’s price. The cost of borrowing Bitcoin remains relatively low, creating opportunities for market-neutral arbitrage. The long-to-short margin ratio on OKX shows longs outweighing shorts by a factor of 15, the lowest level in over three months. Bitcoin options market suggests a neutral stance between bullish and bearish sentiments. The lack of bullish momentum in Bitcoin can be attributed to higher inflation outlook and weaker economic growth projections by the US Federal Reserve, making investors more risk-averse. Overall market sentiment remains subdued despite whales increasing exposure through Bitcoin margin longs.

news.headline: Faraday Future to release Q4 and full year 2024 earnings on March 27, 2025, with an earnings call at 5:00 p.m. PT.
news.summary: Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. (NASDAQ: FFAI), a California-based global shared intelligent electric mobility ecosystem company, will release its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024 after the market closes on Thursday, March 27, 2025. The company will conduct an earnings call at 5:00 p.m. Pacific Time (8:00 p.m. Eastern Time) on the same day for interested investors and other stakeholders.

news.headline: Lamb Weston declares $0.37 quarterly dividend per share, payable on May 30, 2025, to shareholders on record as of May 2.
news.summary: In Eagle, Idaho, the Board of Directors of Lamb Weston Holdings, Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of $0.37 per share of their common stock (NYSE: LW). Shareholders as of May 2, 2025, will receive the payment on May 30, 2025. Lamb Weston is a global provider of frozen potato goods to restaurants and retailers, showcasing 75 years of industry leadership and innovation.

news.headline: DHT Holdings, Inc. filed its 2024 Form 20-F with the SEC in Bermuda on March 20, 2025. NYSE:DHT.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, DHT Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:DHT) based in Hamilton, Bermuda, submitted its 2024 annual report on Form 20-F to the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

news.headline: Trump urges rate cuts after tariffs introduction, Fed leaves rates at 4.25%-4.50%. 3 ETFs to watch in face of uncertainty.
news.summary: President Donald Trump is once again calling on the Federal Reserve to decrease interest rates, pushing for swift action in the wake of new tariffs being introduced. This adds an additional layer of uncertainty to the already complex economic situation, as the Fed opted to maintain rates at 4.25%-4.50%, expressing growing concerns about inflation and weakening economic growth. The Federal Reserve’s shift to a more accommodating stance on quantitative tightening, coupled with lowered growth projections and heightened inflation expectations, has alerted market participants. On the positive side, three Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) have been identified as potential beneficiaries if interest rates remain stable or decrease. One of these ETFs is the Vanguard Ultra-Short Treasury ETF (NASDAQ: VGUS), which offers a hedge against interest rate fluctuations by focusing on short-term Treasury debt securities. This ETF is considered appealing as interest rate volatility looms, providing investors with reduced interest rate risk exposure.

news.headline: Ron Keating appointed to Hayward Holdings, Inc. Board of Directors, bringing total board members to 10, with nine independence.
news.summary: Hayward Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: HAYW) based in Charlotte, North Carolina announced the appointment of Ron Keating as the newest independent director on their Board. This decision brings the total number of directors on the Hayward Board to 10, with nine of them being independent. Hayward's President, CEO, and Director, Kevin Holleran, mentioned that Keating's appointment adds valuable global operational, financial, and water industry expertise to the company. This move aims to enhance the board's capabilities and strategic insights.

news.headline: Emergent BioSolutions granted equity award to new hire under Inducement Plan, NYSE:EBS on March 20, 2025.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, Gaithersburg-based public health company Emergent BioSolutions Inc. (NYSE:EBS) declared the issuance of an equity award to a recent employee by its Compensation Committee under the 2023 Inducement Plan. Emergent BioSolutions Inc. is renowned for providing essential protective and life-saving solutions globally.

news.headline: BioRestorative Therapies to reveal 2024 financial results on March 27, 2025. Conference call scheduled post-release.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, BioRestorative Therapies, Inc. revealed that it will publish its financial results for 2024 after market hours on March 27, 2025. BioRestorative, a company traded on NASDAQ under the symbol BRTX, specializes in regenerative medicine using stem cell-based treatments. Dyne Therapeutics granted inducement equity awards to its new CFO Erick J. Lucera under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).
news.summary: In Waltham, Massachusetts, on March 20, 2025, Dyne Therapeutics, Inc. (Nasdaq: DYN), a company in the clinical stage focusing on developing therapeutics for individuals with genetically driven neuromuscular conditions, disclosed the granting of equity awards to its new Chief Financial Officer, Erick J. Lucera. The Compensation Committee of Dyne authorized these inducement awards, which were provided to Mr. Lucera upon his employment with Dyne. This action was taken in adherence to Dyne's 2024 Inducement Stock Incentive Plan as required by Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).
